purpose: Define which MES modeling objects should be controlled by PLM, referenced by PLM, or left under MES ownership.

rules.plmMaster: PLM owns design intent, versioning, approval, release package, and sync payload.
rules.plmReference: PLM reads MES data for selection, validation, and reconciliation, but does not create or overwrite the MES master object.
rules.mesOwned: MES remains the system of record. PLM may store external references and sync results only.

plmMaster.scope: Product name, version, type, production type, product group, owner, gross die, bank/bin/reticle bindings, route/BOM bindings.
Product/version controlled process sequence, step name, step version, process stage, and process groups.
Product-specific route content and step controls.
Product/version controlled enter/exit actions, reason codes, and step changes.
Product/version controlled branch and rework paths.
Released process route identity, version, and product binding.
Product classification when used by PLM release governance.

mesOwned: EquipmentRecipe body and equipment/chamber execution logic
syncGuardrails: Do not write to production MES before a sandbox write path is confirmed.
Do not persist cookies, tokens, or passwords in repository files.
Only approved and frozen PLM release packages can generate MES sync payloads.
Use idempotency keys based on object name, version, and ECN number.
Start with ProductDef and product-route/BOM binding before enabling lower-level controls.
EquipmentRecipe is validation/reference only in the first implementation phase.
